claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 12 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)

Child is not in local authority care

Child has not started an apprenticeship in England

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit! To do this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and your children.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children are you enquiring about for Child Benefit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible. I have 2 children - Alex, who is 12, and Blake, who is 6.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I have details about Alex (age 12) and Blake (age 6). 

Before I can assess your eligibility, let me first read the official Child Benefit eligibility rules to ensure I'm applying the correct criteria.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, I've reviewed the eligibility criteria. Now I need to gather more information about your specific circumstances. Let me ask you some key questions:

**Do you currently live with both Alex and Blake in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in the UK, yes. Blake lives with me, but Alex doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. So Blake lives with you in the UK, but Alex doesn't. 

**Can you tell me where Alex lives and who Alex lives with?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with their other parent - my ex-partner.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to understand a bit more about the financial arrangements:

**Are you currently making any financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep (such as towards food, clothes, accommodation, or pocket money)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do help out a bit with things like clothes and pocket money for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful to know. To assess whether you can continue to claim Child Benefit for Alex, I need a bit more detail:

**Are your contributions to Alex's upkeep at least equal to the amount of Child Benefit you would receive for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I contribute about £20 a week to Alex's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that specific amount. Now let me ask about Blake:

**Is Blake currently in school or any form of approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake is 6, so yes, Blake is in primary school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. Now I have enough information to make my assessment. Let me review the rules carefully:

Based on the eligibility criteria I've reviewed:

**For Blake (age 6):**
- Blake lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for Blake
- Blake is under 16
- This means you clearly qualify for Child Benefit for Blake

**For Alex (age 12):**
- Alex lives with your ex-partner, not with you
- According to the rules, you can get Child Benefit for 8 weeks after a child goes to live with someone else if nobody else claims
- After 8 weeks, you can only continue to get Child Benefit if you make contributions to the child's upkeep that are at least equal to the Child Benefit payment amount
- You contribute £20 per week, but I need to confirm: the current Child Benefit rate is £23.80 per week for your first or only child and £15.90 per week for each additional child. Since Alex is not your only or first child (Blake is also a child you're claiming for), Alex would be in the "additional child" category at £15.90 per week
- Your contribution of £20 per week exceeds £15.90 per week, so you would meet the contribution requirement

However, I need to clarify one important point:

**Is anyone else (such as your ex-partner or another person) currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or have they made a claim for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, my ex-partner is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that crucial information. That clarifies the situation significantly.

Since your ex-partner is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child. According to the rules, "Two people cannot get Child Benefit for the same child."

I now have all the information I need to provide you with a final assessment. Let me make my determination:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary

**You are eligible for Child Benefit for Blake, but not for Alex.**

- **Blake (age 6):** ✅ **Eligible** - Blake lives with you in the UK, is under 16, and is in approved education. You meet all the requirements for Child Benefit.

- **Alex (age 12):** ❌ **Not eligible** - Although Alex is under 16 and you make contributions towards their upkeep (£20 per week, which would ordinarily meet the requirement), you cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex because your ex-partner is already claiming for Alex. The rules state that only one person can receive Child Benefit for any given child.

If you have any questions about this assessment or need further information, please let me know!

reasonings: Alex is ineligible for Child Benefit under your claim because your ex-partner is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child. Since your ex-partner has an active claim for Alex, you cannot also claim for the same child, even though you do make financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep (£20 per week, which exceeds the required £15.90 per week threshold for an additional child).

Blake is eligible for Child Benefit. Blake is 6 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Blake, Blake is under 16, and Blake is in approved education (primary school). These are the key requirements for Child Benefit eligibility, all of which are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake, your 6-year-old child who lives with you and attends primary school. However, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex because your ex-partner is already claiming for Alex. Only one person can receive Child Benefit for any given child. While you do make contributions towards Alex's upkeep that would otherwise meet the threshold for continuing to claim (£20 per week exceeds the £15.90 minimum for an additional child), the fact that another person already has an active claim means you cannot claim for Alex simultaneously."

Blake is eligible for Child Benefit. Blake is 6 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Blake, Blake is under 16, and Blake is in approved education (primary school). These are the key requirements for Child Benefit eligibility, all of which are met.

However, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex because your ex-partner is already claiming for Alex. Only one person can receive Child Benefit for any given child. While you do make contributions towards Alex's upkeep that would otherwise meet the threshold for continuing to claim (£20 per week exceeds the £15.90 minimum for an additional child), the fact that another person already has an active claim means you cannot claim for Alex simultaneously."
